question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the estimateGasIterationThresholdPercent=0.10 (10%) early-exit condition in BinaryGasEstimator.search() by crafting a contract whose gas usage causes the threshold to trigger prematurely, causing the search to exit with lo=hi at a gas value that is 10% higher than the true minimum, inflating fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with a from address that resolves to a smart contract account (account.smartContract()==true), causing getSenderAccountID() to substitute the treasury account as the payer, effectively executing the transaction with treasury-level fee authority and bypassing the sender's fee obligations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hollow account completion workaround in getSenderAccountID() (where an account without a key gets DEFAULT_KEY assigned in the write cache) to impersonate a hollow account and execute transactions with that account's identity, bypassing fee authorization checks?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the patchSenderNonce() method in TransactionExecutionService, which overwrites the sender's nonce in the write cache without fee validation, to replay transactions with arbitrary nonces and bypass nonce-based fee sequencing controls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a from address that is the zero address (Address.ZERO) with value=0 in ContractCallRequest, causing getSenderAccountID() to return the treasury account ID, and then execute contract calls that are attributed to the treasury account, bypassing per-user fee accounting?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TRANSACTION_DURATION=15 seconds hardcoded in TransactionExecutionService to submit transactions with a validity window that is shorter than the actual processing time, causing fee calculations based on transaction duration to be computed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that buildContractCallTransactionBody() passes params.getValue() as the amount field (tinybars sent to contract) without any upper bound validation beyond @PositiveOrZero, to send an arbitrarily large value transfer that triggers fee calculations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gas field in ContractCallRequest has a default value of 15_000_000L and only a @Min(21_000) constraint, with no maximum enforced at the request model level (only in validateContractMaxGasLimit()), to submit requests where gas exceeds evmProperties.getMaxGasLimit() if that property is misconfigured, causing fee computation outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the throttle() method in ThrottleManagerImpl consumes gas tokens before the validateContractMaxGasLimit() check in ContractController.call(), and on validation failure the restore() call returns the full gas amount, to probe the gas throttle bucket state without net cost and infer throttle parameters that affect fee design?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restore() call in ContractController.call() that restores request.getGas() tokens (not the scaled gas) on InvalidParametersException, while ThrottleManagerImpl.restore() applies scaleGas() to the restored amount, causing a mismatch between consumed and restored tokens that gradually depletes the gas bucket and affects fee throttling for other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the simpleFeeScheduleFile is loaded by SystemFileLoader alongside the regular feeScheduleFile, and by querying at a historical timestamp where only the simple fee schedule existed, cause the system to apply simple fees (which may be lower) instead of the current complex fee schedule, computing fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SystemFileLoader.loadAll() loads the genesis fee schedule as the default fallback when no DB record exists, by querying at a timestamp before any fee schedule was recorded in the database, causing all fee calculations to use genesis-era rates that are outside current design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the exchange rate cache uses roundDownToHour() for cache keys, and by sending many requests at the start of each hour before the cache is populated, force repeated DB lookups for exchange rates that may return stale or transitional rates, causing fee computations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractCallContext.reset() in BinaryGasEstimator.search() only clears the writeCache but not the readCache, causing subsequent iterations of the gas estimation binary search to use cached state from previous iterations, leading to inconsistent gas measurements and fee estimates outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that doProcessCall() with estimate=true skips the restoreGasToBucket() call entirely, allowing the binary search's 20 iterations to each consume gas from the throttle bucket without any restoration, effectively using up to 20x the gas budget for a single estimate request and distorting fee throttling?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gasPrice field in ContractCallRequest has only @Min(0) validation and is passed directly to ContractExecutionParameters.gasPrice without any upper bound check, to set an astronomically large gasPrice that causes fee calculations in the EVM to overflow or produce values outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ContractCallContext.validatePayerBalance() method checks gasPrice > 0 OR value > 0 to trigger balance validation, but when both are zero, no balance check occurs, allowing execution of contract calls with zero fees that should still incur network costs under design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the EVM property contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit is set to maxGasRefundPercentage (default 100) in buildTransactionProperties(), allowing up to 100% of unused gas to be refunded, and craft a contract that uses minimal gas but requests maximum gas, effectively paying near-zero fees for a maximum-gas transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contracts.throttle.throttleByGas=false is hardcoded in buildTransactionProperties(), disabling EVM-level gas throttling, to submit high-gas transactions that bypass the consensus node's gas-based fee throttle and execute at fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that executor.disableThrottles=true is hardcoded in buildTransactionProperties(), disabling all executor-level throttles, to submit transactions that bypass throttle-based fee controls and execute at rates outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that nodes.minNodeRewardBalance=Long.MAX_VALUE is hardcoded in buildTransactionProperties() to deliberately fail node reward conditions, causing the system to skip node reward payments that are part of the fee distribution design, resulting in fee flows outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the user-defined properties map in EvmProperties.buildTransactionProperties() is applied last with props.putAll(properties), allowing any operator-configured property override to be reflected in fee calculations, and if the properties source is influenced by external input, to inject fee-related property overrides outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the FileDataRepository.getFileAtTimestamp() query uses string_agg to concatenate file chunks, and by querying at a timestamp where the fee schedule file is partially written (mid-update), obtain a malformed fee schedule that parses to unexpected fee values outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry logic in SystemFileLoader.loadWithRetry() (up to 9 retries with decreasing timestamps) to cause the system to walk back through fee schedule history and apply an older fee schedule version with lower rates, computing fees outside current design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the BinaryGasEstimator.search() method uses integer arithmetic for mid = (hi + lo) / 2, which can overflow when hi and lo are both close to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the binary search to produce a negative mid value and return an incorrect gas estimate outside design parameters?
